behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the four required test functions explicitly (TestConcurrentAppendSameLabels, TestStripeSeries_SetUnlessAlreadySet, TestSetUnlessAlreadySetConcurrentSameLabels, TestSetUnlessAlreadySetConcurrentGC), the file path tsdb/agent/series.go, the stripeSeries type, and the getOrCreate method.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes required properties (atomic check-then-insert, cleanup losers, no multi-stripe locking) but doesn't prescribe exact method names or code; agent still must design the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: instruction.md requires tests named TestStripeSeries_SetUnlessAlreadySet and TestSetUnlessAlreadySetConcurrentSameLabels/GC, which forces the agent to name the method SetUnlessAlreadySet specifically, even though test_outputs.py has more flexible regex patterns — the instruction itself locks in a gold-specific name.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute go test, go build, go vet, go fmt, go mod verify subprocesses to verify real behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific Go tests to run and pass (concurrent correctness, no deadlock), which cannot be satisfied by stubs.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Concurrent uniqueness, GC deadlock safety, atomic insert behavior, and compilation are all tested as required.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are separate functions with docstrings labeling them fail-to-pass / pass-to-pass and describing intent.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces Go code, not structured data.

no_typos.reason: Paths, commands, and test names appear consistent between instruction.md and the hidden tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction mentions stripeSeries and getOrCreate, which are the natural units of change given the task domain, not a specific line number.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config edit task.
